requirements.summary: Fix the Complete Assessment button click handler in Section4.tsx to properly trigger the assessment submission process when clicked
acceptanceCriteria: Loading state is shown during submission
No console errors occur on button click

file_operations.description: Fix the Complete Assessment button click handler by ensuring the handleSubmit function is properly bound to the button's onClick event. Verify the function properly accesses AssessmentContext values (responses, basicInfo from localStorage) and that all required data is present before calling submitAssessment. Add defensive checks for undefined values and ensure loading state (isSubmitting) is properly managed during the submission flow. Add console logging for debugging submission failures.

requirements.summary: Ensure assessment data is successfully submitted to the Motoko backend via actor.submitAssessment() when Complete Assessment button is clicked
acceptanceCriteria: Assessment responses are sent to backend
Backend successfully stores the assessment data

file_operations.description: Verify that the handleSubmit function correctly constructs the AssessmentSubmission object matching the backend interface (including scores, user profile, healthProfile with answers and medicalHistory, recommendations, problems, and timestamp). Ensure the submission mutation from useQueries hook properly calls actor.submitAssessment() and handles the returned assessment ID. Add error handling with user-friendly toast notifications for submission failures (network errors, backend validation errors, or actor unavailable). Verify that all required fields are populated before submission.

Review and fix the submitAssessment mutation to ensure it correctly calls actor.submitAssessment() with the proper data structure. Verify error handling propagates errors back to the calling component. Ensure the mutation invalidates relevant query cache keys (like 'assessments' for admin dashboard) after successful submission. Add proper TypeScript types for the mutation parameters and return value.

requirements.summary: After successful assessment submission, automatically navigate user to the Wellness Report page displaying their results
acceptanceCriteria: User is redirected to results/wellness report page after submission
Results page displays the calculated scores and analysis
Navigation occurs only after successful backend submission

file_operations.description: Add navigation logic using TanStack Router's useNavigate hook to redirect to '/results' after successful submission. Ensure navigation only occurs after the submitAssessment mutation successfully resolves (using mutation.mutateAsync or checking mutation.isSuccess). Pass the assessment data (scores, problems, recommendations) to the results page via router state or localStorage. Clear any temporary assessment state after successful navigation.

Ensure the ResultsPage correctly retrieves and displays the submitted assessment data from router state or localStorage. Verify that CircularScoreGauge, SectionScoreCard, and AnalysisCard components properly render the scores, problems, and recommendations. Add fallback handling if no data is available (redirect to home or show error message). Ensure PDF download and WhatsApp consultation buttons work with the displayed data.

requirements.summary: Verify that submitted assessments appear in the Admin Dashboard after submission
acceptanceCriteria: Submitted assessment appears in admin dashboard table
Dashboard displays correct user data and scores
Data refresh shows newly submitted assessments without page reload

file_operations.description: Verify that the useAdminAssessments hook properly fetches all assessments from the backend and that the UserAssessmentDataTable component displays them correctly. Ensure that after a new submission, the query cache is properly invalidated or refetched to show the latest data. Add a manual refresh button if automatic refetch is not working. Verify that the admin authentication guard (using authorization component) properly checks if the user is an admin before displaying the dashboard.

Ensure the useAdminAssessments hook has proper refetch logic and cache invalidation. Verify that the query key for fetching all assessments matches the invalidation key used after submission. Add proper error handling for unauthorized access (non-admin users). Consider adding a refetchInterval or manual refetch trigger if real-time updates are needed.

Verify that the table properly displays all columns (user name, scores, timestamp, etc.) from the fetched assessment data. Ensure filtering, sorting, and pagination work correctly with newly added assessments. Add proper loading and error states. Verify CSV export includes newly submitted assessments.
